original_abstract: In recent years, the field of artificial intelligence has undergone a profound paradigm shift driven by the emergence of generative models capable of high-level performance in both supervised and unsupervised learning contexts. Generative AI has established new benchmarks for addressing complex real-world challenges across diverse domains, including medical diagnostics, image-to-image translation, natural language processing, and multimodal data fusion. This paper provides a systematic review and analysis of contemporary advancements in Generative AI, offering a detailed exploration of architectural innovations and application-specific models. The study highlights the transformative impact of large language models on text generation and investigates the success of generative techniques in synthesizing high-fidelity visual and interdisciplinary content. A primary contribution of this work is the coherent synthesis of recent breakthroughs—ranging from Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) and Variational Autoencoders (VAEs) to Transformers and Diffusion models—while mapping the future trajectory of the field. Finally, the paper addresses the critical necessity of Responsible AI, discussing the ethical considerations and sustainability principles essential for the ongoing development and ethical deployment of generative technologies.
humanized_abstract: Generative models have been a paradigm shift in the field of artificial intelligence (AI) in the recent years. Many generative models have shown high-level performance for a wide range of supervised and unsupervised learning problems. Using generative models have shown great promises in many real-world applications, such as medical image analysis, image-to-image translation, language modeling and multimodal learning, etc. This paper provides a survey of the state-of-the-art research works on Generative AI and delves into the new architectures and models proposed for specific applications. It also gives a comprehensive analysis on the impact of large language models for text generation and the success of generative models in producing highly realistic results for both visual and multi-modal data. This paper is one of the pioneering works that not only provides a comprehensive survey of the various generative models such as Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs), Variational Autoencoders (VAEs), Transformers and Diffusion models but also shows the integration of the recent advancements in this field and also gives an outlook of the future research works in the Generative AI. In addition, this paper also highlights the necessity of the “Responsible AI” and also lists the ethical and sustainable aspects that should be considered for the design, implementation and usage of generative AI models.
